message:
🐛 Fix tile rocket scroll: guard scrollToBottom for alternate screen (#190)

* 🐛 Fix broken trackpad scroll + improve active tab contrast

- Remove aggressive wheel handler that blocked ~95% of trackpad events
  (120ms throttle + momentum detection killed normal scrolling)
- Keep minimal handler that only blocks during font-size changes
- Rocket scroll prevention now relies on TerminalWriter scrollTop
  save/restore during screen-clearing writes
- Fix active tab text/icon contrast: white text and light icons on
  blue background instead of dark fg.muted colors
- Update terminal-scroll.test.ts to match new handler behavior (14 tests)

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Fix tile jitter from unnecessary re-renders on poll intervals

- useSwarmStatus: return prev state reference when poll data unchanged
  (was creating new object every 5s, triggering full TerminalView re-render)
- ConnectionStatus: skip setApiReachable when value already matches
  (10s health check was also triggering unnecessary re-renders)

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* ✨ Auto-summarize tiles every 5 prompts + improve scroll stabilization

- Replace one-time auto-summarize with per-tile prompt counter that
  triggers AI summarize every 5 prompts, keeping tab and tile titles
  current with the session's activity
- Distinguish manual vs auto summarize: manual clicks set userRenamed
  to lock the name; auto-summaries leave it false for continuous updates
- Broaden TerminalWriter scroll stabilization from screen-clear-only
  to all writes, preventing rocket scroll when user scrolls up to
  read history in non-tmux terminals
- Extract SUMMARIZE_TIMEOUT_MS constant on server side

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* 🐛 Fix tile rocket scroll: guard scrollToBottom for alternate screen

Three fixes to prevent rocket scroll in tile mode:

1. Guard scrollToBottom() on active tab change (line ~1019) — skip when
   terminal is in alternate screen mode (tmux, vim, Claude Code) since
   TerminalWriter already enforces scrollTop=0.

2. Guard scrollToBottom() in scaleTiles() (line ~1158) — same alternate
   screen check prevents fighting with TerminalWriter's scroll lock.

3. Add suppressScroll=true around scaleTiles fit() calls — blocks wheel
   events during fitAddon.fit() to prevent momentum-scroll-induced jumps,
   matching the existing pattern used for font-size changes.

Also includes uncommitted TerminalWriter improvements: alternate screen
detection, scrollTop=0 enforcement, and debug logging for scroll corrections.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
